# BRAVE Framework

**B**rainstorm **R**eflect **A**pproach **V**alue **E**stimate

A mental framework for validating that we're adding value to products and teams on each task.

When new work enters the queue, or an old task enters the queue after sitting in the backlog for a few weeks, use BRAVE to validate the work that needs to be done.

---

## Brainstorm — What work are we being asked to do?

- Listen to the customer
- Ask clarifying and probing questions
- Look at real scenarios together
- Think out loud
- Reflect back what you're hearing to ensure comprehension
- Describe how you might proceed to solve the problem, and ask the customer if that approach makes sense
- Try to come away with an initial understanding of the other 4 themes of BRAVE:
  - How will I reflect this back to ensure we're all on the same page?
  - How would I begin to approach building a solution?
  - What is the value of this feature and why are we doing it now vs later?
  - How much time will this take and are there any major risks that could bite us?

---

## Reflect — Did we understand one another?

- Share your understanding of the work that needs to be done using a **different** medium or artifact than you used in the brainstorm discussion. For example, if you just talked during the brainstorm, you might reflect using a whiteboard. If you brainstormed with the aid of a whiteboard, you might reflect using a clickable prototype, quick functional demo, or a checklist on the card.
- Reflecting back understanding using a medium different from the original conversation makes it fresh and helps the receiver spot gaps or errors that the first conversation missed.
- Each person on your team will have a slightly different comprehension style. Some will be more visual, others will be more auditory, others will be tactile, etc. Reflecting understanding in more than one way helps the whole team comprehend and recognize gaps in their understanding.

---

## Approach — How do we plan to tackle this work?

- What's our hypothesized technical approach?
- Can we simplify it further?
- What initial spike will we do to test the waters and feel good about our direction/estimates?
- What are the relevant existing patterns in the software? Should this new feature conform to them or start a new pattern?
- Can we leverage existing UI components? If not, can we make this a reusable UI component?
- If there's an error or the user makes a mistake, how would they know? Should we add any error states or a way for the user to recover?
- How might we test part way through the effort to ensure we're on the right track?
- How will we measure success after we ship?
- How might we make this feature more measurable by adding certain hooks or data etc?

---

## Value — What is the value this work is intended to achieve?

- What value is the business hoping to get out of this work?
- What value do we hope the user/customer will experience as a result of this work?
- What is the value of this work for the current sprint? Is it essential or nice-to-have?
- What is the value of this work for the current project phase? Is it essential or nice-to-have?
- Should we optimize our work on this task for speed to deliver, quality of delivery, or learning?

---

## Estimate — What are the costs and risks associated with this work?

- How much pairing do we think should occur for this task?
- How many person hours do we think this task is?
- How much calendar time do we think this will take in light of past performance, dependencies, feedback loops, collaborator availability?
- What are the biggest risks, how likely are they, and how risky are they?
- Is this feature all-or-nothing or is there incremental value we can ship if we don't complete the feature as currently defined? Can we ship the smaller increment and create a card for the rest?
- Is it worth it? If it's doubtful, advise the customer but trust them to make the decision. No venture is without risk.

---

## When to Revalidate

Revalidating is needed when:

- Going to exceed the estimate
- Spike returns a different result than expected
- Customer asks for a change
